note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the use of Positron Emission Tomography (PET) imaging to study the biodistribution of a radiotracer in mice. The goal is to analyze the uptake and distribution of the radiotracer in various organs such as the brain, heart, kidney, and spleen.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A mouse is anesthetized using 2% Isoflurane and 98% Oxygen at 0.5 - 1.5 L/min.",
    "The mouse is placed in a PET scanner for imaging.",
    "The radiotracer, labeled with F18, is administered intravenously.",
    "Data collection begins immediately after administration."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "F18-labeled radiotracer",
    "Mice",
    "PET scanner",
    "Anesthesia machine"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "PET scanner",
    "Anesthesia machine",
    "Computer interface for data analysis"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Admin Time: 00:00:00",
    "Assay Activity: 60.002585283016 uCi",
    "Residual Activity: 2.37 uCi",
    "Cal #: 472"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Transition from anesthesia to imaging",
    "Transition from data collection to analysis"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Graphs displaying biodistribution data for different organs",
    "Numbers representing activity levels in each organ"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "Use of body conforming animal molds for automated image analysis",
    "Intravenous administration of the radiotracer"
  ]
}
